items.title: Tigresses suffer heavy defeat in T20 World Cup warm-up
items.summary: Bangladesh national women's team suffered a crushing 68-run defeat in their opening warm-up match of the upcoming ICC Women's T20 World Cup at the Loughborough University, Loughborough on Saturday.

items.title: Women's T20 World Cup 2026 warm-up: Kapp, Wolvaardt take SA past Ireland
items.summary: Scotland thumped Netherlands by 58 runs, Athapaththu helped SL beat Pakistan, and NZ eased past Bangladesh

items.title: Need to rebuild relationship at the ICC with other nations - Tamim Iqbal
items.summary: A day before the Bangladesh Cricket Board elections, the former national captain who is the front-runner to get elected as BCB president, opened up about issues of nepotism, the need for strengthening ties with India and more in an exclusive chat

items.title: Tamim hints at bypassing DPL bylaws to resolve Brothers Union crisis
items.summary: Brothers Union's Dhaka Premier League (DPL) status could yet be up for discussion despite tournament bylaws dictating relegation following their boycott of Friday's match against Agrani Bank Cricket Club at BKSP.

items.title: Aussies touch down in Dhaka for ODI, T20I series
items.summary: A majority of Australia's squad arrived in Dhaka on Friday afternoon ahead of their three-match ODI and T20I series against Bangladesh, with the remaining members of the touring party scheduled to reach the capital later in the night.

items.title: Brothers set for relegation; Mushfiqur reaches 12,000 List A runs
items.summary: Brothers Union, who have only two points and are already at the bottom of the 12-team standings, will now be relegated because of the walkover, according to CCDM officials.

items.title: Nepal apologises following first-ever 'timed out' dismissal in women's T20
items.summary: Sri Lanka's Angelo Mathews was the first batter to be 'timed out' in international cricket at the 50-over World Cup in 2023 when his helmet strap broke as he was coming on to bat against Bangladesh and asked for a replacement, which delayed him

items.title: Nepal, Malaysia, Hong Kong, Oman qualify for Asian Games men's cricket competition
items.summary: The four teams will join Afghanistan, Bangladesh, India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka and Japan in the ten-team competition starting September 24

items.title: Bangladesh bowling coach Shaun Tait steps down over family reasons
items.summary: BCB says the decision has been reached amicably

items.title: Dilara's fastest fifty guides Bangladesh to victory over Netherlands
items.summary: Dilara scored her fifty off 22 balls, the fastest by a Bangladesh batter in women's T20I

items.title: ICC delegation visits Bangladesh to 'review developments' on BCB's governance, electoral matters
items.summary: ICC's Dr Mohammed Moosajee and Tavengwa Mukuhlani will submit their findings and observations to the ICC around the BCB's governance issues

items.title: Shaun Tait resigns as Bangladesh fast bowling coach
items.summary: After 12 months in the role, he is stepping down to prioritise family time

items.title: Kathryn Bryce's all-round effort takes Scotland to tri-series win
items.summary: Scotland finished as the table-toppers after beating Netherlands, with three wins in four matches and one match between Bangladesh and Netherlands to go

items.title: Bangladesh recall Mosaddek after four years for ODIs against Australia
items.summary: Nurul Hasan also returns to the ODI squad for the first two games. Mahidul Islam and Afif Hossain have been left out

items.title: New Zealand home summer to start with blockbuster all-format India tour
items.summary: The New Zealand men's side will also host Sri Lanka for an all-format tour, while the women will host Bangladesh for three T20Is and as many ODIs

items.title: Sultana, Moni help Bangladesh bounce back against Scotland
items.summary: Fast bowler Moni starred with a career-best four-for to trigger Scotland's collapse, in their chase of 153

items.title: Siegers fifty and three-for help Netherlands make history
items.summary: They won their first ever women's T20I against Bangladesh by eight runs
